name: Senda Ría de Navia — circular from the old town of Navia
name_original: Senda Ría de Navia — circular desde el casco antiguo de Navia
region_intro: Hiking around Navia offers diverse natural scenery, characterized by its extensive coastline, river estuaries, and varied landscapes. The region features numerous beaches, such as Playa de Barayo and Playa de Navia, providing opportunities for coastal walks. Inland, the terrain includes areas suitable for exploring natural features like the Cascada Firbia-Oneta, offering a mix of coastal and riverside hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around Navia The most popular hiking route is Senda Ría de Navia — circular from the old town of Navia, a 3.3 miles (5.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 24 minutes to complete. This easy route offers views along the Navia estuary. Another top favourite among local hikers is Puerto de Vega Harbor – Beautiful walk loop from Puerto de Vega, a moderate 6.5 miles (10.5 km) path. This route explores the coastal landscape around Puerto de Vega. Local hikers also love the View of Frejulfe Beach – Playa de Frexulfe loop from Puerto de Vega, a 4.7 miles (7.6 km) trail leading through coastal scenery and past Frexulfe Beach, often completed in about 2 hours 3 minutes. Hiking around Navia is defined by coastal paths, river estuaries, and diverse beaches. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging coastal treks. The routes in Navia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 130 reviews. More than 800 hikers have used komoot to explore Navia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Navia?
A: Navia offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 40 trails documented on komoot. These routes cater to different preferences, ranging from easy strolls to more challenging treks.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Navia?
A: The hiking routes in Navia are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.7 stars from over 130 reviews. More than 800 hikers have explored the diverse terrain, often praising the scenic coastal paths and river estuaries.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Navia has 17 easy hiking routes. A great option for a relaxed outing is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1048385853/senda-ria-de-navia-circular-from-the-old-town-of-navia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda Ría de Navia — circular from the old town of Navia&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 3.3-mile (5.3 km) trail that offers pleasant views along the Navia estuary.

Q: Are there challenging hikes for experienced hikers in Navia?
A: For those seeking a more demanding experience, Navia features 7 difficult hiking routes. One such challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10502917" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de Navia – Playa de Frexulfe loop from Playa de Frexulfe&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 13 miles (21 km) and includes significant elevation changes.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Navia?
A: Yes, many routes in Navia are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1048385853/senda-ria-de-navia-circular-from-the-old-town-of-navia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda Ría de Navia — circular from the old town of Navia&lt;/a&gt; is a popular example of a circular trail.

Q: Are there hikes that feature waterfalls in the Navia region?
A: Yes, the Navia area is home to natural attractions like waterfalls. You can explore routes that lead to or pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2540246" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oneta Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;, also known as Cascada Firbia-Oneta, offering a refreshing natural spectacle.

Q: What coastal walks or beach trails are available in Navia?
A: Navia is renowned for its extensive coastline and beautiful beaches. You can enjoy coastal paths and trails that lead past beaches such as Playa de Barayo, Playa de Navia, and Frexulfe Beach. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14985946" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Frejulfe Beach – Playa de Frexulfe loop from Puerto de Vega&lt;/a&gt;, which offers stunning coastal scenery.

Q: What historical or cultural sites can I explore while hiking in Navia?
A: Beyond natural beauty, Navia offers historical points of interest. You can find routes that pass near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3710397" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Luarca Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;, or explore the charming fishing village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4804569" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Luarca&lt;/a&gt;. The Castro of Coaña, an ancient fort, is also a notable historical site in the region.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of the harbor?
A: Yes, you can find routes that provide picturesque views of local harbors. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14985951" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Vega Harbor – Beautiful walk loop from Puerto de Vega&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.5-mile (10.5 km) path that explores the coastal landscape around Puerto de Vega, offering lovely harbor vistas.

Q: Are there any other notable natural monuments or viewpoints to discover?
A: Absolutely. Besides the beaches and waterfalls, you can discover other natural monuments and viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5058429" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Frejulfe Beach&lt;/a&gt; is a popular highlight, and you might also encounter unique rock formations or secluded coves like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3710352" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portizuelo Beach&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/812347" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de Moro&lt;/a&gt;.
tips:
Navia is a prosperous council located in the western Asturian marina. Bathed to the north by the waters of the Cantabrian Sea, it is limited to the east by the council of Valdés, to the west by that of Coaña and to the south by that of Villayón.
The town of Navia is the capital of the council. It is strategically located at the mouth of the homonymous river, at the bottom of a navigable estuary, which has been and continues to be one of the keys to its strength.

The Navia City Council is located presiding over the Plaza de las Armas, it is a building dating from the 19th century that has 2 floors as well as a ground floor. On the façade stands out the shield that is framed with figures of lions and just below are the flags.

A very nice and quiet beach, also known as Andés beach. There are restaurants and bars next door. The path to the beach goes through a forest of aromatic pines and eucalyptus trees. There is also a lake, known as "Vega de Arenas".
